records.chapter: Chapter 75 – Making up the Night Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1430 - Murazim ibn Hakim al-Azdi narrated that He said:
"I was ill for four months and did not perform any of the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers during that time. I mentioned this to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)
Imam (as) said: 'There is no obligation on you to make them up; indeed, the condition of the sick is not like that of the healthy. Whatever overwhelms you, Allah (swt) is more deserving of offering an excuse for it.'".
records.arabicText:
1430 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُرَازِمِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ اَلْأَزْدِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَرِضْتُ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ لَمْ أُصَلِّ نَافِلَةً فِيهَا فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي مَرِضْتُ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ لَمْ أُصَلِّ نَافِلَةً فَقَالَ
«لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ قَضَاءٌ إِنَّ اَلْمَرِيضَ لَيْسَ كَالصَّحِيحِ كُلُّ مَا غَلَبَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ فَاللَّهُ أَوْلَى بِالْعُذْرِ فِيهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1430 - Murazim ibn Hakim al-Azdi narrated that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I was ill for four months and did not perform any of the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers during that time. I mentioned this to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)
Imam (as) said: 'There is no obligation on you to make them up; indeed, the condition of the sick is not like that of the healthy. Whatever overwhelms you, Allah (swt) is more deserving of offering an excuse for it.'".

records.chapter: Chapter 75 – Making up the Night Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1431 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrates that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"I asked him about a man who became ill and thus did not perform the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers.
Imam (as) said: 'O’ Muhammad, they are not obligatory. If he makes them up, it is a good deed he performs, and if he does not, there is no obligation upon him.'"
records.arabicText: 1431 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مَرِضَ فَتَرَكَ اَلنَّافِلَةَ فَقَالَ «يَا مُحَمَّدُ لَيْسَتْ بِفَرِيضَةٍ إِنْ قَضَاهَا فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ يَفْعَلُهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1431 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrates that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about a man who became ill and thus did not perform the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers.
Imam (as) said: 'O’ Muhammad, they are not obligatory. If he makes them up, it is a good deed he performs, and if he does not, there is no obligation upon him.'"

records.chapter: Chapter 75 – Making up the Night Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1432 - Sulaiman ibn Khalid asked Imam (as) about making up the Witr prayer after midday.
Imam (as) said: "Make it up as a single (Witr) always, just as it was missed."
records.arabicText: 1432 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ: عَنْ قَضَاءِ اَلْوَتْرِ بَعْدَ اَلظُّهْرِ فَقَالَ «اِقْضِهِ وَتْراً أَبَداً كَمَا فَاتَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1432 - Sulaiman ibn Khalid asked Imam (as) about making up the Witr prayer after midday.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Make it up as a single (Witr) always, just as it was missed."

records.chapter: Chapter 75 – Making up the Night Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1433 - Hammad ibn Uthman asked Imam (as) and said:
"I wake up after missing the Witr until the night. How do I make it up?"
Imam (as) said: "Like for like."
records.arabicText: 1433 - وَ سَأَلَهُ حَمَّادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ فَقَالَ لَهُ: أُصْبِحُ عَنِ اَلْوَتْرِ إِلَى اَللَّيْلِ فَكَيْفَ أَقْضِي فَقَالَ «مِثْلاً بِمِثْلٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1433 - Hammad ibn Uthman asked Imam (as) and said:
"I wake up after missing the Witr until the night. How do I make it up?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Like for like."

records.chapter: Chapter 75 – Making up the Night Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1434 - Hariz narrated that Imam (as) said,
"My father (Imam (as)) would sometimes make up twenty Witr prayers in a single night."
records.arabicText: 1434 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ حَرِيزٌ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رُبَّمَا قَضَى عِشْرِينَ وَتْراً فِي لَيْلَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1434 - Hariz narrated that Imam (as) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (Imam (as)) would sometimes make up twenty Witr prayers in a single night."

records.chapter: Recognizing the Dawn and the Saying Upon Seeing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1436 - Ali ibn Atiyyah narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"The true Fajr (dawn) is the one that, when you see it, appears spread out horizontally, like the whiteness of the Surā river."
records.arabicText: 1436 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَطِيَّةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «اَلْفَجْرُ هُوَ اَلَّذِي إِذَا رَأَيْتَهُ كَانَ مُعْتَرِضاً كَأَنَّهُ بَيَاضُ نَهَرِ سُورَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1436 - Ali ibn Atiyyah narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The true Fajr (dawn) is the one that, when you see it, appears spread out horizontally, like the whiteness of the Surā river."

records.chapter: Recognizing the Dawn and the Saying Upon Seeing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1437 - It has been narrated:
"The time of Fajr (morning prayer) begins when the true dawn appears and spreads light horizontally in a good way”.
records.arabicText: 1437 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ وَقْتَ اَلْغَدَاةِ إِذَا اِعْتَرَضَ اَلْفَجْرُ فَأَضَاءَ حَسَناً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1437 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The time of Fajr (morning prayer) begins when the true dawn appears and spreads light horizontally in a good way”.

records.chapter: Recognizing the Dawn and the Saying Upon Seeing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1438 - Ammaar ibn Musa al-Sabati narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"When the Fajr (dawn) rises, say:
Alḥamdu lillāhi Fāliqi al-Iṣbāḥ, subḥāna Allāhi Rabbi al-masā’i wa aṣ-ṣabāḥ.
Allahumma ṣabbiḥ āla Muḥammad bi-barakatin wa ‘āfiyatin wa surūrin wa qurrati ‘ayn.
Allahumma innaka tunzilu bil-layli wa an-nahār mā tashā’, fa-anzil ‘alayya wa ‘ala ahl baytī min barakati as-samāwāti wa al-arḍi rizqan ḥalālan ṭayyiban wāsi‘an tughnīnī bihi ‘an jamī‘i khalqik.
'Praise be to Allah (swt), the Splitter of the dawn! Glory be to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of evening and morning! O’ Allah (swt), bless the family of Muhammad with blessing, health, joy, and a source of comfort. O’ Allah (swt), You send down at night and during the day whatever You will, so send upon me and my family from the blessings of the heavens and the earth, a lawful, good, and abundant provision that makes me free of need from all Your creation.'"
records.arabicText:
1438 - وَ رَوَى عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُوسَى اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «تَقُولُ إِذَا طَلَعَ اَلْفَجْرُ: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَالِقِ اَلْإِصْبَاحِ سُبْحَانَ اَللَّهِ رَبِّ اَلْمَسَاءِ وَ اَلصَّبَاحِ اَللَّهُمَّ صَبِّحْ آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ بِبَرَكَةٍ وَ عَافِيَةٍ وَ سُرُورٍ وَ قُرَّةِ عَيْنٍ اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ تُنْزِلُ بِاللَّيْلِ
وَ اَلنَّهَارِ مَا تَشَاءُ فَأَنْزِلْ عَلَيَّ وَ عَلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتِي مِنْ بَرَكَةِ اَلسَّمَاوَاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضِ رِزْقاً حَلاَلاً طَيِّباً وَاسِعاً تُغْنِينِي بِهِ عَنْ جَمِيعِ خَلْقِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1438 - Ammaar ibn Musa al-Sabati narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the Fajr (dawn) rises, say:
Alḥamdu lillāhi Fāliqi al-Iṣbāḥ, subḥāna Allāhi Rabbi al-masā’i wa aṣ-ṣabāḥ.
Allahumma ṣabbiḥ āla Muḥammad bi-barakatin wa ‘āfiyatin wa surūrin wa qurrati ‘ayn.
Allahumma innaka tunzilu bil-layli wa an-nahār mā tashā’, fa-anzil ‘alayya wa ‘ala ahl baytī min barakati as-samāwāti wa al-arḍi rizqan ḥalālan ṭayyiban wāsi‘an tughnīnī bihi ‘an jamī‘i khalqik.
'Praise be to Allah (swt), the Splitter of the dawn! Glory be to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of evening and morning! O’ Allah (swt), bless the family of Muhammad with blessing, health, joy, and a source of comfort. O’ Allah (swt), You send down at night and during the day whatever You will, so send upon me and my family from the blessings of the heavens and the earth, a lawful, good, and abundant provision that makes me free of need from all Your creation.'"

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1439 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim who reported from one of the Imams (as) that he asked him about sleeping after the Fajr (morning) prayer.
Imam (as) said: "Sustenance is distributed during that hour, and I dislike for a man to sleep during that time."
records.arabicText: 1439 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلنَّوْمِ بَعْدَ اَلْغَدَاةِ فَقَالَ «إِنَّ اَلرِّزْقَ يُبْسَطُ تِلْكَ اَلسَّاعَةَ فَأَنَا أَكْرَهُ أَنْ يَنَامَ اَلرَّجُلُ تِلْكَ اَلسَّاعَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1439 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim who reported from one of the Imams (as) that he asked him about sleeping after the Fajr (morning) prayer.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Sustenance is distributed during that hour, and I dislike for a man to sleep during that time."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1440 - Jabir narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"Indeed, Iblis deploys his soldiers of the night from sunset until the disappearance of twilight and deploys his soldiers of the day from the time the Fajr (morning) light appears until the sun rises." He also mentioned that "the Prophet of Allah (swt) (sw) used to say: 'Increase your remembrance of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, during these two hours, and seek refuge in Allah (swt) from the evil of Iblis and his soldiers, and seek protection for your children during these two hours, for they are times of heedlessness.'"
records.arabicText: 1440 - وَ رَوَى جَابِرٌ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِنَّ إِبْلِيسَ إِنَّمَا يَبُثُّ جُنُودَ اَللَّيْلِ مِنْ حِينِ تَغِيبُ اَلشَّمْسُ إِلَى مَغِيبِ اَلشَّفَقِ وَ يَبُثُّ جُنُودَ اَلنَّهَارِ مِنْ حِينِ يَطْلُعُ اَلْفَجْرُ إِلَى مَطْلَعِ اَلشَّمْسِ» وَ ذَكَرَ «أَنَّ نَبِيَّ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَانَ يَقُولُ» «أَكْثِرُوا ذِكْرَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي هَاتَيْنِ اَلسَّاعَتَيْنِ وَ تَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ شَرِّ إِبْلِيسَ وَ جُنُودِهِ وَ عَوِّذُوا صِغَارَكُمْ فِي هَاتَيْنِ اَلسَّاعَتَيْنِ فَإِنَّهُمَا سَاعَتَا غَفْلَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1440 - Jabir narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Iblis deploys his soldiers of the night from sunset until the disappearance of twilight and deploys his soldiers of the day from the time the Fajr (morning) light appears until the sun rises." He also mentioned that "the Prophet of Allah (swt) (sw) used to say: 'Increase your remembrance of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, during these two hours, and seek refuge in Allah (swt) from the evil of Iblis and his soldiers, and seek protection for your children during these two hours, for they are times of heedlessness.'"

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1441 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The morning sleep (after Fajr) is inauspicious; it drives away sustenance, causes paleness and disfigures the complexion, and alters it negatively. It is the sleep of every unfortunate person. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, distributes sustenance between the rising of Fajr and the rising of the sun, so beware of that sleep."
records.arabicText: 1441 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «نَوْمَةُ اَلْغَدَاةِ مَشُومَةٌ تَطْرُدُ اَلرِّزْقَ وَ تُصَفِّرُ اَللَّوْنَ وَ تُقَبِّحُهُ وَ تُغَيِّرُهُ وَ هُوَ نَوْمُ كُلِّ مَشْئُومٍ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى يَقْسِمُ اَلْأَرْزَاقَ مَا بَيْنَ طُلُوعِ اَلْفَجْرِ إِلَى طُلُوعِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَإِيَّاكُمْ وَ تِلْكَ اَلنَّوْمَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1441 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The morning sleep (after Fajr) is inauspicious; it drives away sustenance, causes paleness and disfigures the complexion, and alters it negatively. It is the sleep of every unfortunate person. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, distributes sustenance between the rising of Fajr and the rising of the sun, so beware of that sleep."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1442 - Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Sleeping at the beginning of the day is wastefulness, the nap at midday is a blessing, sleeping after Asr (afternoon prayer) is foolishness, and sleeping between the two night prayers (Maghrib and Isha) deprives one of sustenance."
records.arabicText: 1442 - وَقَالَ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلنَّوْمُ أَوَّلَ اَلنَّهَارِ خُرْقٌ وَ اَلْقَائِلَةُ نِعْمَةٌ وَ اَلنَّوْمُ بَعْدَ اَلْعَصْرِ حُمْقٌ وَ اَلنَّوْمُ بَيْنَ اَلْعِشَاءَيْنِ يَحْرِمُ اَلرِّزْقَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1442 - Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Sleeping at the beginning of the day is wastefulness, the nap at midday is a blessing, sleeping after Asr (afternoon prayer) is foolishness, and sleeping between the two night prayers (Maghrib and Isha) deprives one of sustenance."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1443 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If you see someone sleeping on their face, wake them up."
records.arabicText: 1443 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ نَائِماً عَلَى وَجْهِهِ فَأَنْبِهُوهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1443 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you see someone sleeping on their face, wake them up."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1444 - Imam (as) said:
"Three things bring forth the dislike (anger) of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: sleeping without fatigue, laughing without wonder, and eating when already full”.
records.arabicText: 1444 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «ثَلاَثَةٌ فِيهِنَّ اَلْمَقْتُ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ نَوْمٌ مِنْ غَيْرِ سَهَرٍ وَ ضَحِكٌ مِنْ غَيْرِ عَجَبٍ وَ أَكْلٌ عَلَى اَلشِّبَعِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1444 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Three things bring forth the dislike (anger) of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: sleeping without fatigue, laughing without wonder, and eating when already full”.

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1445 - A Bedouin came to the Prophet (sw) and said:
"O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), I used to have a strong memory but now I have become forgetful."
The Prophet (sw) asked: "Did you use to take a midday nap (Qailulah)?"
The man replied: "Yes."
The Prophet (sw) then asked: "And did you stop doing that?"
The man answered, "Yes."
The Prophet (sw) said: "Resume it."
The man did so and his memory returned.
records.arabicText: 1445 - وَ أَتَى أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ إِنِّي كُنْتُ ذَكُوراً وَ إِنِّي صِرْتُ نَسِيّاً فَقَالَ «أَ كُنْتَ تَقِيلُ» قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ «وَ تَرَكْتَ ذَاكَ» قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ «عُدْ» فَعَادَ فَرَجَعَ إِلَيْهِ ذِهْنُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1445 - A Bedouin came to the Prophet (sw) and said:
"O’ Messenger of Allah (sw),
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I used to have a strong memory but now I have become forgetful."
The Prophet (sw) asked: "Did you use to take a midday nap (Qailulah)?"
The man replied: "Yes."
The Prophet (sw) then asked: "And did you stop doing that?"
The man answered, "Yes."
The Prophet (sw) said: "Resume it."
The man did so and his memory returned.

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1446 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There are five types of people who cannot sleep: one who is anxious about shedding blood, the possessor of a great amount of wealth who lacks a trustworthy guardian, one who spreads lies and false accusations among people to gain a worldly benefit, the person burdened with a large amount of wealth but has none himself, and one who deeply loves someone and fears separation from them."
records.arabicText: 1446 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «خَمْسَةٌ لاَ يَنَامُونَ اَلْهَامُّ بِدَمٍ يَسْفِكُهُ وَ ذُو اَلْمَالِ اَلْكَثِيرِ لاَ أَمِينَ لَهُ وَ اَلْقَائِلُ فِي اَلنَّاسِ اَلزُّورَ وَ اَلْبُهْتَانَ عَنْ عَرَضٍ مِنَ اَلدُّنْيَا يَنَالُهُ وَ اَلْمَأْخُوذُ بِالْمَالِ اَلْكَثِيرِ وَ لاَ مَالَ لَهُ وَ اَلْمُحِبُّ حَبِيباً يَتَوَقَّعُ فِرَاقَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1446 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There are five types of people who cannot sleep: one who is anxious about shedding blood, the possessor of a great amount of wealth who lacks a trustworthy guardian, one who spreads lies and false accusations among people to gain a worldly benefit, the person burdened with a large amount of wealth but has none himself, and one who deeply loves someone and fears separation from them."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1447 - It is narrated:
"Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed Allah (swt) nourishes and provides drink to the fasting person during their sleep."
records.arabicText: 1447 - وَ رُوِيَ: «قِيلُوا فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ يُطْعِمُ اَلصَّائِمَ فِي مَنَامِهِ وَ يَسْقِيهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1447 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed Allah (swt) nourishes and provides drink to the fasting person during their sleep."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1448 - It is narrated,
"Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed the Shaytan does not take a nap."
records.arabicText: 1448 - وَ رُوِيَ: «قِيلُوا فَإِنَّ اَلشَّيْطَانَ لاَ يَقِيلُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1448 - It is narrated,
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed the Shaytan does not take a nap."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1449 - Imam (as) said:
"Sleeping in the morning (after dawn) is an omen of misfortune; it deprives one of sustenance and pales the complexion. The manna and quails used to descend upon the Children of Israel between the time of dawn until sunrise, so whoever slept during that hour would not receive their share. When they woke up and did not find their portion, they were compelled to ask and seek provision”.
records.arabicText: 1449 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «نَوْمُ اَلْغَدَاةِ شُؤْمٌ يَحْرِمُ اَلرِّزْقَ وَ يُصَفِّرُ اَللَّوْنَ وَ كَانَ اَلْمَنُّ وَ اَلسَّلْوَى يَنْزِلُ عَلَى بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ مَا بَيْنَ طُلُوعِ اَلْفَجْرِ إِلَى طُلُوعِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَمَنْ نَامَ تِلْكَ اَلسَّاعَةَ لَمْ يَنْزِلْ نَصِيبُهُ فَكَانَ إِذَا اِنْتَبَهَ فَلاَ يَرَى نَصِيبَهُ اِحْتَاجَ إِلَى اَلسُّؤَالِ وَ اَلطَّلَبِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1449 - Imam (as) said:
"Sleeping in the morning (after dawn) is an omen of misfortune; it deprives one of sustenance and pales the complexion.
records.thaqalaynMatn: The manna and quails used to descend upon the Children of Israel between the time of dawn until sunrise, so whoever slept during that hour would not receive their share. When they woke up and did not find their portion, they were compelled to ask and seek provision”.

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1450 - Imam al-Ridha (as) said regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "By those who distribute commands"
He (Imam (as)) explained: "The angels distribute the sustenance of the children of Adam from the time of dawn until sunrise. Therefore, whoever sleeps during this period sleeps away their sustenance."
records.arabicText: 1450 - وَقَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «فَالْمُقَسِّمٰاتِ أَمْراً» قَالَ «اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ تُقَسِّمُ أَرْزَاقَ بَنِي آدَمَ مَا بَيْنَ طُلُوعِ اَلْفَجْرِ إِلَى طُلُوعِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَمَنْ يَنَامُ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُمَا يَنَامُ عَنْ رِزْقِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1450 - Imam al-Ridha (as) said regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "By those who distribute commands"
He (Imam (as)) explained:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The angels distribute the sustenance of the children of Adam from the time of dawn until sunrise. Therefore, whoever sleeps during this period sleeps away their sustenance."

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1451 - Muammar ibn Khallad narrated from Imam al-Ridha (as) that when he was in Khurasan, after performing the Fajr prayer, he would sit in his prayer place until the sun rose. Then, a pouch containing miswaks (tooth-cleaning sticks) would be brought to him, and he would use them one by one. Afterward, he would be given frankincense, which he would chew for some time before leaving it aside. He would then be brought a Qur'an, which he would read from.
records.arabicText: 1451 - وَ رَوَى مُعَمَّرُ بْنُ خَلاَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: كَانَ وَ هُوَ بِخُرَاسَانَ إِذَا صَلَّى اَلْفَجْرَ جَلَسَ فِي مُصَلاَّهُ إِلَى أَنْ تَطْلُعَ اَلشَّمْسُ ثُمَّ يُؤْتَى بِخَرِيطَةٍ فِيهَا مَسَاوِيكُ فَيَسْتَاكُ بِهَا وَاحِداً بَعْدَ وَاحِدٍ ثُمَّ يُؤْتَى بِكُنْدُرٍ فَيَمْضَغُهُ ثُمَّ يَدَعُ ذَلِكَ فَيُؤْتَى بِالْمُصْحَفِ فَيَقْرَأُ فِيهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1451 - Muammar ibn Khallad narrated from Imam al-Ridha (as) that when he was in Khurasan, after performing the Fajr prayer,
records.thaqalaynMatn: he would sit in his prayer place until the sun rose. Then, a pouch containing miswaks (tooth-cleaning sticks) would be brought to him, and he would use them one by one. Afterward, he would be given frankincense, which he would chew for some time before leaving it aside. He would then be brought a Qur'an, which he would read from.

records.chapter: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1452 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever remains seated in their place of prayer after the Fajr prayer until the sun rises, Allah (swt) will protect them from the Fire."
records.arabicText: 1452 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «مَنْ جَلَسَ فِي مُصَلاَّهُ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَلْفَجْرِ إِلَى طُلُوعِ اَلشَّمْسِ سَتَرَهُ اَللَّهُ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1472 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from Al-Sadiq from his father (as) who said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) had a staff with a pointed end, which he would lean on, and he would take it out on the two Eids and pray towards it”.
records.arabicText: 1472 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «كَانَتْ لِرَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَنَزَةٌ فِي أَسْفَلِهَا عُكَّازٌ يَتَوَكَّأُ عَلَيْهَا وَ يُخْرِجُهَا فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ يُصَلِّي إِلَيْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1472 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from Al-Sadiq from his father (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (sw) had a staff with a pointed end, which he would lean on, and he would take it out on the two Eids and pray towards it”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1453 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said: "The prayers of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) are obligatory, and the prayer of the eclipse (Salat al-Kusoof) is also obligatory."
records.arabicText: 1453 - رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «صَلاَةُ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ فَرِيضَةٌ وَ صَلاَةُ اَلْكُسُوفِ فَرِيضَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1453 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prayers of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) are obligatory, and the prayer of the eclipse (Salat al-Kusoof) is also obligatory."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1454 - From Zurarah, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"The prayer of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) with the Imam is a Sunnah, and there is no prayer before or after them on that day until noon (zawal)."
records.arabicText: 1454 - عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «صَلاَةُ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ مَعَ اَلْإِمَامِ سُنَّةٌ وَ لَيْسَ قَبْلَهُمَا وَ لاَ بَعْدَهُمَا صَلاَةٌ ذَلِكَ اَلْيَوْمَ إِلَى اَلزَّوَالِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1454 - From Zurarah, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prayer of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) with the Imam is a Sunnah, and there is no prayer before or after them on that day until noon (zawal)."
